utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , john . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey . well , relatively speaking , okay . good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good to see you as well . so i know the nurse told you about dax . i'm gon na tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so john is a 61-year-old male with a past medical history significant for kidney stones , migraines and reflux , who presents with some back pain . so john , what's going on with your back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i'm feeling a lot of the same pain that i had when i had kidney stones about two years ago , so i'm a little concerned .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . and so wh- what side of your back is it on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , honestly , it shifts . it started from the right side and it kinda moved over , and now i feel it in the left side of my back .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , um , how many days has this been going on for ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: the last four days .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and is ... is the pain coming and going ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , at first it was coming and going , and then for about the last 48 hours , it's been a constant , and it's ... it's been pretty bad .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and what have you taken for the pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: tylenol , but it really does n't seem to help .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . okay . and do you have any blood in your urine ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , uh , it ... i think i do . it's kind of hard to detect , but it does look a little off-color .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and have you had , uh , any other symptoms like nausea and vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , if i'm doing something i'm ... i'm , uh , like exerting myself , like climbing the three flights of stairs to my apartment or running to catch the bus , i feel a little dizzy and a little light headed , and i ... i still feel a little bit more pain in my abdomen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , so let- let's talk a little bit about your ... your migraines . how are you doing with those ? i know we started you on the imitrex a couple months ago .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been pretty diligent about taking the meds . i ... i wan na make sure i stay on top of that , so i've been pretty good with that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so no issues with the migraine ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: none whatsoever .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how about your ... your acid reflux ? how are you doing with ... i know you were making some diet changes .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i've been pretty good with the diet , but with the pain i have been having, it has been easier to call and have something delivered. so i have been ordering a lot of take-out and fast food that can be delivered to my door so i don't have to go out and up and down the steps to get it myself. but other than that , it's been pretty good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . are you staying hydrated ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . okay , well , let's go ahead and , uh , i know the nurse did a review of systems , you know , with you , and i know that you're endorsing some back pain and a little bit of dizziness , um , and some blood in your urine . any other symptoms ? you know , muscle aches , chest pain ... uh , body aches , anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have some body aches because i think i'm ... i'm favoring , um , my back when i'm walking because of the pain , like i kinda feel it in my muscles , but not out of the ordinary and not surprised 'cause i remember that from two years ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , let's go ahead and ... and look at your vital signs today . hey , dragon ? show me the blood pressure . yeah , so your blood pressure's a little high today . that's probably because you're in some pain , um , but let ... let me just take a listen to your heart and lungs , and i'll let you know what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so on ... on physical exam , you do have some , uh , cda tenderness on the right-hand side , meaning that you're tender when i ... when i pound on that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and your abdomen also feels a little tender . you have some tenderness of the palpation of the right lower quadrant , but other than that , your heart sounds nice and clear and your lungs are clear as well . so let's go ahead and take a look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? show me the creatinine . so we ... we drew a creatinine when you came in here because i was concerned about the kidney stones . it ... it is uh ... it is up slightly , which might suggest that you have a little bit of a obstruction there of one-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . of the stones . okay ? hey , dragon . show me the abdominal x-rays . okay , and there might be a question of a ... uh , of a stone there lower down , uh , but we'll wait for the official read there . so the , uh , abdominal x-rays show a possible kidney stone , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and plan for you . so , for your first problem , your back pain , i think you're having a recurrence of your kidney stones . so i wan na go ahead and order a ct scan without contrast of your abdomen and pelvis . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i'm also gon na order you some ultram 50 milligrams as needed every six hours for pain . does that sound okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? order ultram 50 milligrams every six hours as needed for pain . and i want you to push fluids and strain your urine . i know that you're familiar with that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your next problem , for your migraines , let's continue you on the imitrex . and for your final problem , uh , for your reflux , uh , we have you on the protonix 40 milligrams a day . do you need a refill of that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: actually , i do need a refill .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon ? order a refill of protonix 40 milligrams daily . okay . so the nurse will be in soon , and she'll help you get the cat scan scheduled . and i'll be in touch with you in ... in a day or so .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: perfect .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: if your symptoms worsen , just give me a call , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: take care .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey ... hey , dragon ? finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 61-year-old male with a history of kidney stones, migraines, and gastroesophageal reflux presenting with back pain.
Kidney stones
The patient reports back pain similar to his previous kidney stone episode from two years ago.
The doctor asks for the location of the back pain.
The pain shifted from the right side to the left side of the back.
The doctor asks about the duration of the symptoms.
The pain has been present for four days.
The doctor asks if the pain is intermittent or constant.
The pain was initially intermittent but has been constant for the last 48 hours.
The doctor asks what the patient has taken for pain.
The patient has taken Tylenol without relief.
The doctor asks if the patient has hematuria.
The patient reports off-color urine, suggesting hematuria.
The doctor asks about nausea and vomiting.
The patient reports nausea, vomiting, dizziness, and lightheadedness with exertion, along with abdominal pain.
Migraines
The doctor asks about the patient's migraine status and adherence to Imitrex.
The patient is diligent about taking Imitrex.
The doctor asks if there are any issues with migraines.
The patient denies any issues with migraines.
Reflux
The doctor asks about the patient's acid reflux and diet.
The patient reports eating more fast food and takeout due to pain, but is otherwise managing well.
The doctor asks if the patient is staying hydrated.
The patient confirms he is staying hydrated.
The doctor asks about other symptoms including muscle aches, chest pain, and body aches.
The patient reports body aches related to favoring his back due to pain.
The patient has elevated blood pressure, and the doctor performs a heart and lung exam.
Physical exam shows CVA tenderness on the right.
Physical exam shows right lower quadrant abdominal tenderness, while heart and lungs are clear.
Creatinine level is slightly elevated, suggesting possible obstruction.
Abdominal x-ray demonstrates a possible kidney stone.
The doctor orders a CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis without contrast.
Ultram 50 mg every 6 hours as needed for pain.
The doctor advises the patient to push fluids and strain urine.
The patient is taking Protonix 40 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill of Protonix.
The patient confirms he needs a refill of Protonix.
The doctor orders a refill of Protonix 40 mg daily.
The doctor instructs the patient to call if symptoms worsen.
